column1: # Options instances as function parameters — tests that Options-typed
# fn parameters correctly match (or reject) concrete Map arguments.
# Format: expression<TAB>expected result
# --- Basic Options parameter matching ---
# Options with type constraints — map with all fields present
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:String}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1 y:"hi"}
# Options with type constraints — missing required field rejects
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:String}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1}
# Options with type constraints — wrong type for field rejects
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:"hi"}
# Options with type constraints — extra keys allowed (open/subset matching)
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1 y:2}
# --- Options with concrete default values ---
# Concrete defaults — exact same values match
def Opts (make Options {x:1 y:2}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1 y:2}
# Concrete defaults — different values do not match
def Opts (make Options {x:1 y:2}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:10 y:20}
# --- Options with disjunct fields ---
# Disjunct field — integer value matches
def Opts (make Options {x:(Integer or String)}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1}
# Disjunct field — string value matches
def Opts (make Options {x:(Integer or String)}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:"hi"}
# Disjunct field — boolean value does not match
def Opts (make Options {x:(Integer or String)}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:true}
# Disjunct with None — integer value matches
def Opts (make Options {x:(Integer or None)}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1}
# Disjunct with None — None value matches
def Opts (make Options {x:(Integer or None)}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:None}
# --- Options with optional (?) fields ---
# Optional field present — matches
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y?:String}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1 y:"hi"}
# Optional field absent — pattern still requires key, so rejects
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y?:String}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1}
# --- Empty Options ---
# Empty Options — any map matches
def Opts (make Options {}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1 y:2}
# Empty Options — empty map matches
def Opts (make Options {}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {}
# --- Accessing fields from Options-matched parameter in fn body ---
# Dot access on Options-matched map parameter — single field
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:Integer}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Integer] [m . x]] f {x:3 y:4}
# Dot access — compute with two fields
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:Integer}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Integer] [(m . x) add (m . y)]] f {x:3 y:4}
# Dot access — string field
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:String}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [String] [m . y]] f {x:1 y:"world"}
# --- Multiple parameters including Options ---
# Integer + Options params
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer}) def f fn [[n:Integer m:Opts] [Integer] [n add (m . x)]] f 5 {x:3}
# String + Options params
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer}) def f fn [[s:String m:Opts] [String] [s add (m . x)]] f "val:" {x:7}
# Two Options params — access fields from each
def A (make Options {x:Integer}) def B (make Options {y:Integer}) def f fn [[a:A b:B] [Integer] [(a . x) add (b . y)]] f {x:3} {y:7}
# --- Options parameter with multiple fields of same type ---
# Multiple integer fields
def Opts (make Options {a:Integer b:Integer c:Integer}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Integer] [(m . a) add (m . b) add (m . c)]] f {a:1 b:2 c:3}
# --- Options parameter with mixed type and concrete fields ---
# Mix of type constraint and concrete default
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:42}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1 y:42}
# Mix — type field matches, concrete field wrong
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:42}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:1 y:99}
# --- Nested Options scenarios ---
# Options param — compute and return single value
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer y:Integer}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Integer] [(m . x) mul (m . y)]] f {x:10 y:20}
# --- Type hierarchy matching ---
# Number field matches integer (subtype)
def Opts (make Options {x:Number}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:42}
# Scalar field matches string (subtype)
def Opts (make Options {x:Scalar}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:"hi"}
# Scalar field matches integer (subtype)
def Opts (make Options {x:Scalar}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:99}
# Integer field rejects string
def Opts (make Options {x:Integer}) def f fn [[m:Opts] [Map] [m]] f {x:"hi"}
